disclaimer: This dashboard is a decision-support tool based on open-source intelligence (OSINT). It is NOT official guidance. Consult your embassy and local authorities for evacuation decisions.

sections.title: Air Defense / Interceptor Effectiveness
sections.summary: UAE intercept rates remain above 93% for ballistic missiles and 94% for drones. No signs of interceptor depletion yet, but resupply timeline is the key unknown.
sections.analysis: Intercept rates holding steady at 93-98%. The critical variable is interceptor stockpile depth — estimated 800-1000 Patriot-class missiles at war start. At ~150 intercepts/day across all Gulf states, depletion window is Mar 8-13 without resupply. US has announced resupply but logistics are unconfirmed.

sections.title: Attack Volume on UAE
sections.summary: UAE absorbs 57.4% of all GCC-targeted projectiles. Daily drone volume stable at ~120/day. Ballistic missiles plateaued at 16-17/day (Days 8-9). IRGC announced intent to escalate.
sections.analysis: Campaign is NOT de-escalating (+7.1% vs prior report). Ballistic missile plateau at 16-17/day broke the earlier decline trend. Iran's unused cruise missile stockpile (~3,000) remains the biggest wildcard. IRGC publicly stated intent to expand scale and intensity.

sections.title: Airport & Airspace Operations
sections.summary: Emirates cancelled flights Mar 9-15. DXB experiencing repeated temporary closures due to interception debris. Multiple airlines suspended UAE routes.
sections.analysis: Commercial aviation from DXB is severely degraded. Emirates full-week suspension is the clearest signal. Muscat (MCT) remains the viable alternative for outbound flights. Evacuation by air requires land transit to Oman first.

sections.title: Ports & Cargo Continuity
sections.summary: Dubai Customs issued emergency notice rerouting all Jebel Ali cargo through east coast ports (Khorfakkan/Fujairah). Supply chains adapting but degraded.
sections.analysis: Dubai is executing a pre-planned contingency: ships dock on the Gulf of Oman side, containers truck overland ~130-150km to Jebel Ali. Adds cost and time but keeps goods flowing. Bloomberg confirms Hormuz is 'all but closed.' East coast bypass maintains supply chain at reduced capacity.

sections.title: Retail Prices & Consumer Stress
sections.summary: UAE government actively policing price gouging. 4,468 inspections, 449 warnings, Dh176K in fines. Food supplies confirmed adequate. Shelves stocked.
sections.analysis: Economy Minister personally toured Al Awir Central Market (main wholesale hub). Government projecting normalcy and control. 420+ additional inspections planned for Ramadan. Price gouging is happening but being enforced against. No food shortages reported.

sections.title: Oman Border & Muscat Viability
sections.summary: Oman has received only 8 projectiles total, no new attacks for several days. Land border crossing (Hili/Buraimi) operational. Muscat airport functional.
sections.analysis: Iran is deliberately NOT targeting Oman — they need the neutral corridor for diplomacy and trade. Only 8 drones total, no missiles, no recent attacks. This is the primary evacuation route: Dubai → Al Ain → Hili/Buraimi border → Muscat → fly out. Route validated by multiple evacuees.

sections.title: Civilian & Infrastructure Escalation
sections.summary: First confirmed residential hits (not debris). 5 dead in UAE (all foreign nationals). Bahrain desal plant hit. Oil infrastructure war has begun.
sections.analysis: Critical escalation: overnight Day 9 saw direct drone hits on residential buildings (not interception debris). First new fatality since Day 2. Bahrain desal plant hit sets precedent for UAE water infrastructure targeting. Israel and Iran both now targeting oil infrastructure. The 'coercive disruption' vs 'civilian punishment' line is blurring.

sections.title: Market-Implied Conflict Duration
sections.summary: Oil surged 20%+ to $109/barrel. Markets pricing 3-6 weeks minimum conflict. No ceasefire signals. Hormuz closed. Extreme Fear across all risk assets.
sections.analysis: Oil past $100 = market consensus shifted from 'days' to 'extended disruption.' Gold dipping on margin-call liquidation (technical, not fundamental — expect recovery). Trump called oil surge 'small price to pay' but $6/gallon gas will test that. Qatar energy minister warned of $150/barrel. No ceasefire: Araghchi rejected it, Trump demands unconditional surrender.

red.trigger: Cruise missile wave targeting UAE cities

red.note: Bahrain desal hit — UAE not yet

amber.trigger: Second UAE fatality from direct hit
Iran deploys cruise missiles at Gulf cities
